title: Prime count via Sieve of Eratosthenes
prompt: Read a non-negative integer N from standard input (a single line). Use a Sieve of Eratosthenes to count the prime numbers in the closed interval [2, N], and write that count to standard output followed by a single newline. Exit with status 0. The count for N = 0 or N = 1 is 0. N is at most 10000.

input_schema.stdin: <non-negative integer N on one line, 0 <= N <= 10000>

output_schema.stdout: <count of primes p with 2 <= p <= N, as decimal digits>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads N from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability. All other languages read from stdin. The harness adapts per language.
